CalifOrnia
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
March 21, 1990
Pete R. Zaragosa
Sunmer Youth Jobs Coordinator
contra costa county
Pr j,vate Industry Council
2425 Bisso Lane, Suite 100
concord, cA 94520
Re: Your Request for Infomal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-062
Dear Mr. Zaragosa:
.
You are seeking advice on behalf of the Contra costa County
Private Industry council ('tthe industry counciltr) regarding ap-
plication of the mass mailing provisions of the Political 
Reforx0
Act (the ttAct")1 to the industry council's youth jobs program.
Because your request for advice does not refer to a specific
governmental decision, we are treating your question as a request
ior infornal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329 (coPy en-
closed).2
1 Gor".rrr"nt Code Sections 810OO-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the Governnent code unless otherwise indicated. commission
reg'ulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations. Section
ie6oo, et Scg.- A11 references to regulatj-ons are to Title 2, Di-
vision e oi ttre cafifornia code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
inuunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Govern-
nent coie- section g3rra; i ca:'. code of Reqs' section
18 32e (c) (3) . )
The following advice
in your letter of January
conversations wj.th you and
As part of its sunmer
send out a mass rnailing to
names and signatures of a
board of supervisors?
is based on the facts that you provided
18, 1990 and in subsequent telephone
with Robert whatford of your office.
OUESTION
youth progratl, can the industry counci.l
private employers that contains the
city :nayor and nember of the county
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o sacramento cA 95804.0807 . (916) 322'5660


File No. I-9o-o62
Page 2
CONCLUSION
The industry council may send the roailing containing the
nanes and signatures of a city nayor and member of the county
board of supervi-sors to private enployers as part of its summer
youth progran. The city or county Day not pay the postage for the
nailing, nor incur expenses in excess of $50 with regard to the
costs of design, production, or printing of the letter.
FACTS
The federal Job Training Partnership act provides funds to
the county for administration of an enploynent training program.
(The funds are allocated to the state, which distributes them to
the county. ) The industry council functions as the governing body
for the enplol'nent training program for most of contra costa
county (the city of Richnond has its own program). Its governing
board is composed of representatives fron private sector business,
the state office of education, the state rehabilitation depart-
ment, coDmunity based organizations, and local governnent. The
board does not include any elected officeholders.
The industry council receives funds from the private sector
to sponsor a ttsummer Youth Jobs Program, " a program designed to
find young people jobs in the connunity. The industry council
uses Lhese iunds to send a rnailing to the business cornmunity which
encourages private ellptoyers to participate in this progran by
naking jobs available. The letter is targeted by area and
containi the nanes and signatures of the city's nayor, the
district's 
supervisor on the county board of supervisors, the 1o-
cal chamber of commerce president, and the industry council chair.
The letter is copied onto resPective city and/or chanber of con-
Berce letterhead and rnailed to the area's Private etnployers.
ANALYSI S
Afflliatior
Regulation 18901(a) (copy enclosed) provides that a Eass
rnailing nay not be sent within the rneaning of section 89001 if,
anong other things, the iteu sent either features an elected of-
ficei affiliated rrith the agency which Produces or sends the nail-
ing (Regulation 18901(a) (2) (A)), or includes the name or other
reieren6e to an elected officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the rnailing, which is prepared in consultation
wittr ttre elected officer. 
(Regulation 18901(a) (2) (B).)
In order for the nailing to fa1l within the nass Dailing re-
strictions, the elected official nust be affiliated with the
agency that sends the nailing. 
An elected officer is affiliated
wittr in agency when he or she is
... a meBber, officer, or employee of the agency or
a subunit sucb as a comroittee, or has supervisory


File No. I-90-062
Page 3
control over the alrency, or appoints one or more
menbers of the agency.
Regulation 18901 (c) (1)
No elected official sits on the industry council board, nor
exercises supervisory control over, nor appoints one or more
members to, the industry council. As such, the mayor and supervi-
sor whose signatures appear on the nailing sent to local employers
are not rraff iliated'i with the industry council. If the industry
council is the agency which produces and sends the letters, then
the ruayor and supervisor are not traffiliatedtr with the industry
council for purposes of Regulation 18901.3 lsee Ridd1e Advice Let-
ter, I-89-095, copy enclosed. )
costs of d€sl.gn, productJ.on, .Dd PrlDting
Regrulation 18901(a) also provides that a nass nailing may not
be sent within the rneaning of section 89001 if, 
among other
things, the costs of the mailing's distribution is paid for with
public funds (Regulation 18901(a) (3)(A)), or if the costs of the
naj.ling,s design, production, and printing are paid for erith pub-
lic funds and exceed S50.00. (Regulation 18901(a) (3) (B).)
The industry council provides the body of its letter to cit-
ies and chambers of comnerce, who in turn copy the letter onto
their letterheads and nail them to the local private employers.
If the city pays to have the letters nailed, the city -- not
the industry council -- is the agency which [ lproduces or] sends
the nailing,rr and the city's rnayor is the featured elected of-
ficial affiliated with the agency. (Regulation 18901(a) (2)(A).)
Under these circunstances the cost of the mailing's distribution
would be paid for with public funds. (Regulation 18901(a) (3) (A).)
Because the Daitings would be sent to the recipient at his or her
business (Regulation 18901(a) (1)), the nailings would be
considered uass rnailj,ngs under the Act and no uore than 2o0 could
be sent in a single calendar nonth. (Regulation 18901(A) (4).)
ff the industry council pays the postage for nailing the let-
ters to the private enployers, it -- and not the city -- is the
agency r'which produces and sendsir the nailing. 
The Act would not
consider the nailing to be a rrroass roailingrr and subj ect the rnail-
ing to the various provisions of section 89001 and Regnrlation
18901 provided the city incurred costs relating to the design,
production, and printingt of the nailing, in an anount not in
excess of $50. (ReguLation 18901(a) (3) (B) ) ,
3 thi. is so even
nane and signature
lation 18901(c) (2).
if the Eayor and supervisor, by having their
included in the letter, are |tfeaturedtt 
(Regu-
)


FiIe No. I-90-062
Page 4
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this nat-
ter please contact ne at (916) 322-5o9L.
sincere ly,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
